introSection:
Green Lantern was a Bolliger & Mabillard stand-up roller coaster that was previously located at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA. It was relocated from Six Flags Kentucky Kingdom where it operated as Chang from 1997 to 2009.

The ride closed on November 10, 2024. Green Lantern, alongside Kingda Ka, Zumanjaro: Drop of Doom, Parachute Training Center: Edwards AFB Jump Tower and Twister were removed to make way for a “multi world record breaking launched roller coaster”.

sections.title: Chang (1997 - 2009)
sections.content:
Chang was announced in August 1996. According to Kentucky Kingdom, the name "Chang" means "long" in Chinese.

The ride was initially constructed at Kentucky Kingdom by Martin & Vleminckx. When Chang opened on April 4, 1997, it set the record for this type of roller coaster in height, drop, speed, length, and number of inversions. All of these records were formerly held by Mantis at Cedar Point and were later broken by Riddler's Revenge at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

When Six Flags acquired the park some time in 1999, the ride was repainted lime green with violet supports.

In 2006, Chang was repainted to the original yellow but the supports were painted blue. Six Flags announced that Chang would be rethemed to Batman, with T2 being renamed to "Batman: The Ride" and Chang to "Riddler's Revenge." These plans were later cancelled.

The ride was removed in 2009. The "Chang" sign was donated to the National Roller Coaster Museum in Plainview, Texas, USA. This was originally for a new waterpark region named Bonzai Beach, but these plans were cancelled when Six Flags announced plans to dispose of its Kentucky Kingdom property in 2010. When Kentucky Kingdom reopened in 2014, the new owners built a waterpark expansion on the former Chang site.

sections.title: Green Lantern (2011 - 2024)
sections.content:
Shortly after the closure of Six Flags Kentucky Kingdom, parts of Chang arrived at Six Flags Great America. The ride was originally scheduled to open here, but those plans never came into fruition. On September 16, 2010, it was announced that Chang would be relocated to Six Flags Great Adventure for the 2011 season. It replaced Great American Scream Machine, an Arrow Mega-Looper, which was torn down in 2010. The ride was rethemed to the DC Comics superhero Green Lantern (Hal Jordan). The ride was repainted green to go with the new theme, and the vertical loop was left yellow. Green Lantern opened on May 25, 2011.

The storyline of the ride was intended to tie into the Green Lantern film releasing that year, and had the guests confronting Parallax, who in DC Comics is a giant, monstrous parasite that can feed on fear and once possessed Hal Jordan. Because of this, the loop was left its original yellow, for riders were supposed to "fear" the loop.

Six Flags Great Adventure filed for permits to demolish the ride in November 2024 and announced its closure shortly thereafter. The ride last operated on November 10, 2024.

Removal of Green Lantern started later in November. It continued throughout December 2024 into March 2025 when the remaining lift hill segment was removed.

As of October 2025, the ride's trains are in storage on park property.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Green Lantern featured a similar, but not identical layout to that of Mantis and Riddler's Revenge. The ride dropped into the former largest vertical loop in the world. Next, the ride went into a series of elements including a dive loop, a diving turnaround, and an inclined loop. At this point, the ride travelled through the mid-course brake-run. The second half of the ride featured low-to-the-ground curves, and two corkscrews.

Green Lantern was a Bolliger & Mabillard stand-up roller coaster that was previously located at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA. It was relocated from Six Flags Kentucky Kingdom where it operated as Chang from 1997 to 2009.

The ride closed on November 10, 2024. Green Lantern, alongside Kingda Ka, Zumanjaro: Drop of Doom, Parachute Training Center: Edwards AFB Jump Tower and Twister were removed to make way for a “multi world record breaking launched roller coaster”.



Chang was announced in August 1996. According to Kentucky Kingdom, the name "Chang" means "long" in Chinese.

The ride was initially constructed at Kentucky Kingdom by Martin & Vleminckx. When Chang opened on April 4, 1997, it set the record for this type of roller coaster in height, drop, speed, length, and number of inversions. All of these records were formerly held by Mantis at Cedar Point and were later broken by Riddler's Revenge at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

When Six Flags acquired the park some time in 1999, the ride was repainted lime green with violet supports.

In 2006, Chang was repainted to the original yellow but the supports were painted blue. Six Flags announced that Chang would be rethemed to Batman, with T2 being renamed to "Batman: The Ride" and Chang to "Riddler's Revenge." These plans were later cancelled.

The ride was removed in 2009. The "Chang" sign was donated to the National Roller Coaster Museum in Plainview, Texas, USA. This was originally for a new waterpark region named Bonzai Beach, but these plans were cancelled when Six Flags announced plans to dispose of its Kentucky Kingdom property in 2010. When Kentucky Kingdom reopened in 2014, the new owners built a waterpark expansion on the former Chang site.

Shortly after the closure of Six Flags Kentucky Kingdom, parts of Chang arrived at Six Flags Great America. The ride was originally scheduled to open here, but those plans never came into fruition. On September 16, 2010, it was announced that Chang would be relocated to Six Flags Great Adventure for the 2011 season. It replaced Great American Scream Machine, an Arrow Mega-Looper, which was torn down in 2010. The ride was rethemed to the DC Comics superhero Green Lantern (Hal Jordan). The ride was repainted green to go with the new theme, and the vertical loop was left yellow. Green Lantern opened on May 25, 2011.

The storyline of the ride was intended to tie into the Green Lantern film releasing that year, and had the guests confronting Parallax, who in DC Comics is a giant, monstrous parasite that can feed on fear and once possessed Hal Jordan. Because of this, the loop was left its original yellow, for riders were supposed to "fear" the loop.

Six Flags Great Adventure filed for permits to demolish the ride in November 2024 and announced its closure shortly thereafter. The ride last operated on November 10, 2024.

Removal of Green Lantern started later in November. It continued throughout December 2024 into March 2025 when the remaining lift hill segment was removed.

Green Lantern featured a similar, but not identical layout to that of Mantis and Riddler's Revenge. The ride dropped into the former largest vertical loop in the world. Next, the ride went into a series of elements including a dive loop, a diving turnaround, and an inclined loop. At this point, the ride travelled through the mid-course brake-run. The second half of the ride featured low-to-the-ground curves, and two corkscrews.
